title: Impact of Age <50 Versus ≥50 Years on In-Hospital Outcomes in Women With Rheumatoid Arthritis
text:
Study Objective: To evaluate the impact of age on outcomes in women with RA.
Data Source: Cross-sectional analysis of the National Inpatient Sample (NIS) from 2017 to 2022. The NIS is part of the Healthcare Cost and Utilization Project (HCUP) and provides a stratified sample of inpatient discharges from U.S. community hospitals across all payers.
Inclusion Criteria:

Age ≥ 18 years
Sex = female
Rheumatoid Arthritis as primary or secondary diagnosis, using ICD 10 codes: M05 and M06.

Exposure:

Age categories: <50 and ≥50 years

Primary Outcomes:

In-hospital all-cause mortality
Total hospital length of stay (days)
Total charge, inflation adjusted to 2022 ($)
Non-home discharge

Secondary Outcomes:

Mechanical ventilation
Respiratory failure
Sepsis
Pneumonia
Acute myocardial infarction
Stroke
Acute kidney injury
Any major joint arthroplasty

Covariates for Adjustment: All models adjusted for the following:

Patient Characteristics:: Race, income, insurance.
Hospital Characteristics: hospital region, hospital bedsize, hospital location and teaching status, day (weekday/weekend) and type of admission (elective/non-elective; except for in arthroplasty).
Comorbidities: Heart failure, diabetes mellitus, hypertension, chronic kidney disease, obesity, coronary artery disease, hyperlipidemia, anemia, chronic pulmonary disease, atrial fibrillation, coagulopathy, smoking, hypothyroidism, cancer (and prior stroke and prior MI for AMI and stroke).

Statistical Methods:

Univariable Analysis:

Categorical variables: Compared using Pearson’s chi-square test with Rao & Scott adjustment
Continuous variables: Analyzed using the design-based Kruskal–Wallis test, a non-parametric alternative suitable for survey-weighted data

Multivariable Analysis:

Binary outcomes (e.g., mortality): Survey-weighted logistic regression using the quasibinomial family
Continuous outcomes (e.g., LOS, hospital charges): Survey-weighted linear regression using the gaussian family
Results reported as adjusted odds ratios (aORs) or beta coefficients with 95% confidence intervals


Software: All statistical analyses were performed using the R Statistical Language (Version 4.5.0; R Foundation for Statistical Computing, Vienna, Austria), incorporating survey-weighted procedures via the survey package to account for the complex sampling design of NIS.
